Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a list of activities in order though not necessarily strict order but we consider all pairs where one eventually follows the other in at least one execution

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to create a temporal profile dictionary that includes pairs of activities as tuples and their average time and standard deviation The times are in seconds

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have 10 activities SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the process is described as a sequence we can assume that the activities occur in the order given but not necessarily strictly consecutive there might be other activities in between

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will create a dictionary with some representative pairs We should include pairs that are directly connected like SS to OP as well as pairs that are farther apart like SS to DT or OP to AS

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to assign realistic average times and standard deviations

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will think about the typical time between activities in a supply chain

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Supplier Selection SS to Order Placement OP This might be a short time maybe a few days Let us say average 2 days and standard deviation of 1 day

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Order Placement OP to Receive Components RC This is the lead time for shipping Could be weeks Say average 14 days and standard deviation of 3 days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Receive Components RC to Quality Inspection QI Probably a short time maybe a few hours Say average 4 hours and standard deviation of 1 hour

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Quality Inspection QI to Component Assembly CA Could be a day or so Say average 1 day and standard deviation of 12 hours

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Component Assembly CA to Product Testing PT might be a few hours to a day Say average 12 hours and standard deviation of 6 hours

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Product Testing PT to Packaging PK might be short a few hours Say average 6 hours and standard deviation of 2 hours

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Packaging PK to Warehouse Storage WS might be immediate but could be a few hours Say average 2 hours and standard deviation of 1 hour

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Warehouse Storage WS to Distribution DT could vary Maybe average 5 days and standard deviation of 2 days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Distribution DT to After Sales Support AS could be months Say average 30 days and standard deviation of 10 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We also need pairs that are not directly connected For example from SS to RC the time would include the time from SS to OP and OP to RC

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For simplicity we can assume that the times between non consecutive activities are roughly the sum of the consecutive ones but with some additional variation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will create a dictionary with the consecutive pairs and a selection of non consecutive pairs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For non consecutive pairs we sum the averages and compute the standard deviation as the square root of the sum of variances assuming independence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to be careful with units all in seconds

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now for the one step apart pairs and the two step apart pairs and longer range pairs the average time is the sum of the consecutive averages and the variance is the sum of variances

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can include these 25 pairs That should be a representative subset

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We should ensure that the dictionary is syntactically correct
